behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is parser/segmentation logic at a single local boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is only about splitting a command string; no OS/network/subprocess/AI dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multi-line shell block (assignments + command) and assert it returns distinct statement segments in order, rather than one merged segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure boundary is handoff between segmentation and trigger classification; unit-testing either alone can miss this. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no AI output dependence, no multi-agent sequence, so not System/Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PostToolUse hook classification path end-to-end in-process with a multi-line script containing assignments then `gh pr create`; assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is also a cross-module invariant (segmentation + trigger matcher + command-type discrimination). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: behavior text does not require real subprocess/API execution of `gh`; it is classification of command text only, so Integration remains the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same in-process PostToolUse classification pipeline with assignments + `gh issue create`; assert result is non-PR trigger and does not enter PR review path.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between trigger detection, identifier reconstruction, and persistence module/filesystem adapter. REAL-INFRA: local file persistence is enough; no real external API/subprocess needed by behavior text. Rejected System because text asks for reconstruct-and-persist state, not validating real `gh` process or network responses.
behaviors.test_description: Using a temp directory/state path, invoke PostToolUse with a PR-create-trigger fixture containing PR identifier inputs; assert persisted state file exists and contains the reconstructed PR identifier field expected by the review loop.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an inter-module contract test (writer hook -> reader/enforcer hook). Unit tests of either side can miss schema/field mismatches. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond local in-process file read/write, and no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp state location, run PostToolUse path that writes review-gate state, then invoke pre-tool enforcement hook against that state; assert it successfully parses state and enforces gate behavior (e.g., blocks/permits according to stored values).
